mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and rising consumer price indices indicate stagflationary demand shocks that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive returns dominate when both series move in the predicted stagflationary direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month expansions in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening economic output that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive during post-shock recovery periods when INDPRO rises, or the mechanism yields a negative Rank IC in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with a rising federal funds rate indicate aggressive monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs and discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances, or the combined proxy effect shows no statistical edge (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and consumer base, supporting higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in the majority of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.3 million units signal robust construction demand and consumer confidence in long-term economic stability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 65% of months where HOUST surpasses 1300.0 units, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in over 60% of late-cycle instances where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5 percent, or the hypothesis yields a negative Rank IC in regime-specific walk-forward validation.
